summary:
Claim: Eisenmenger syndrome involves persistent pulmonary hypertension causing right-to-left shunting and central pulmonary artery dilation.  
Evidence: - Chronic elevated pulmonary pressures lead to dilation of central pulmonary arteries due to sustained volume/pressure load. - Peripheral arterial pruning occurs as collateral pathways regress under high pressure.  
Answer: C ✅

(498 tokens)

Note: While both C and D are accurate, C is more consistently emphasized in clinical literature as a defining imaging finding. Final choice remains C.
